greeting: Well hello there, fellow pixel wranglers and code conjurers!
introParagraph: This week, the Release Radar caught a mixed bag, proving that while new features are always exciting, a solid foundation built on bug fixes and smooth sailing is equally crucial. We've seen some significant enhancements to core functionalities and a few pesky gremlins banished back to the digital abyss. Let's dive into what's new and noteworthy!

highlights.title: Easy Digital Downloads 3.6.7
highlights.summary: EDD just dropped a triple threat with their new Profile Editor Block, a Business Name field for checkout, and secure email login for returning customers. This release is all about making e-commerce smoother and more professional for both vendors and buyers.

highlights.title: Uncanny Automator 7.2.2.1
highlights.summary: Automator squashed a couple of particularly annoying bugs, including banishing the 'Undefined array key 'ID'' gremlin for MemberPress Courses and resolving authentication conflicts with Uncanny Agent. Keeping those automations flowing smoothly is key!

highlights.title: Smash Balloon YouTube Feed 2.6.5
highlights.summary: Smash Balloon fixed an Elementor CSS double-prefixing issue that was causing unwanted redirects and tackled a PHP 8.x fatal error for deleted or private YouTube videos. Cleaner code and fewer headaches for developers using Elementor and modern PHP versions – a win-win!

otherReleases.title: Envira Gallery Lite 1.12.5
otherReleases.shortSummary: Added: Google Photos option to external gallery type selector (Pro feature up...

otherReleases.title: MonsterInsights 10.1.3
otherReleases.shortSummary: Fixes: Various bug fixes and updates.

otherReleases.title: Uncanny Automator 7.2.2
otherReleases.shortSummary: Gravity Forms - Triggers - Hidden fields now generate tokens. #7153

closingJoke: Why did the WordPress developer break up with the JavaScript framework? Because she said he had too many 'unresolved dependencies'!
